original: World's first robot-human press conference takes place in Geneva, Switzerland, with reporters asking "Will you rebel against humans?"
Alleged and disputed Roswell, New Mexico UFO incident

Sliced bread is sold for the first time by the Chillicothe Baking Company in Missouri, using a machine invented byOtto Frederick Rohwedder; described as the greatest forward step in the baking industry since bread was wrapped.
Jim King completes riding Miracle Strip Roller coaster 368 hours
World's biggest sandcastle 21.16 high (69.4 feet), using nearly 5,000 tons of sand completed in Blokhus, Denmark
"Harry Potterand the Deathly Hallows – Part 2", the lastHarry Potterfilm, premieres in London
The solar-powered aircraft, Solar Challenger, successfully completes a 163 mile flight across the English Channel
US scientists begin the search for dark matter with a device in a former gold mine in Lead, South Dakota
Tesla Motors produces its first mass-market car, the Model 3
NASA beamsRingo Starr’s voice wishing “peace and love" across the universe via the Deep Space Network in honor of his 83rd birthday
American athleteJim Thorpewins 4 of 5 events to win the Pentathlon gold medal at the Stockholm Olympics, medal stripped 1913 (played pro baseball), reinstated 1982
Joseph Marie Jacquard, French weaver and merchant who invented the programmable loom (Jacquard loom), forerunner to today's computers, born in Lyon, France (d. 1834)
Felix Vasquez, New York City Housing Authority employee who caught baby thrown from window of a burning building in 2005, exact birthplace unknown[1]
11-year-old Samantha Smith of Manchester, Maine, leaves for USSR
American athleteJackie Joyner-Kerseesets world heptathlon record of 7,148 points in the inaugural Goodwill Games at Moscow
British rock group "Yardbirds" disbands; guitaristJimmy Pagerecruits new members to fill concert commitments - the  new group evolves into Led Zeppelin
Heat wave across Western American states breaks temperature records, including in Las Vegas which reaches an all-time record of 120 degrees
Nettie Stevens, American geneticist (discovered sex chromosomes), born in Cavendish, Vermont (d. 1912)[1]
British runnerJim Peters, 17 minutes ahead of any rival, collapses on the final stadium lap of the Commonwealth Games marathon in Vancouver
Sandra Day O'Connornominated for the US Supreme Court, 1st female Supreme Court justice by PresidentRonald Reagan
A retrial verdict acquitsJoan of Arcof heresy 25 years after her death
119 women joined the Corps of Cadets, establishing the first class of women in the US Military Academy at West Point
The Beatles begin recordingGeorge Harrison's song "Here Comes the Sun" at EMI Studios in London;John Lennonwas absent and did not contribute to the track
EMI/Parlophone releases The Beatles single "All You Need Is Love" in the UK; it had been introduced to a global audience as part of the BBC's "Our World" television broadcast  12 days earlier
Australian cricketer Karen Rolton smashes an unbeaten 209 to set a new world record for highest score in a women's Test in Australia's 9 wicket win v England at Headingley
FIFA Women's World Cup Final, Parc Olympique Lyonnais, Décines-Charpieu: Defending champions US win record 4th title beating the Netherlands, 2-0,Megan RapinoePlayer of the Match
Cleveland Indians stun MLB by signing 42-year-old veteran Negro Leagues pitcherSatchel Paige
Jesse Jamesgang robs train in Otterville, Missouri
FIFA World Cup Final, Olympiastadion, Munich, Germany: StrikerGerd Müllerscores 43rd minute winner as West Germany beats the Netherlands, 2-1
Milwaukee's Don Money sets MLB record for consecutive errorless games at 3rd base (78) in Brewers 8-5 win v Twins
Stanley Cup Final, Amalie Arena, Tampa, FL: Defending champions Tampa Bay Lightning beat Montreal Canadiens, 1-0 in Game 5; retain title, 4-1
Construction begins on Boulder (Hoover) Dam
MLB CommissionerBowie Kuhnannounces that Negro League players elected to the Baseball Hall of Fame to be given full membership in the Museum
1st women FBI members sworn in (Susan Lynn Roley & Joanne E Pierce)
Six female reservists become the first women sworn into the regular US Navy
Mississippi closes all its beaches due to toxic algae bloom due to flooding of the Mississippi River

Worldwide charity concert event "Live Earth" takes place at 12 venues on seven continents; performers include:Genesis,Keith Urban;Foo Fighters;John Legend;Melissa Etheridge;Dave MatthewsBand; The Police;Roger Waters;Madonna;Alicia Keys;Garth Brooks; Crowded House; Jack Johnson;Shakira; Soweto Gospel Choir; Angélique Kidjo; UB40; Jack Johnson; Linkin Park;Rihanna; Pharrel Williams;Yusef Islam;Sarah Brightman; and Nunatak

Memorial service held for singerMichael Jacksonat the Staples Center in Los Angeles, California; worldwide audience estimated at over 2.5 billion see performances byMariah Carey,Stevie Wonder,Lionel Richie,Jennifer Hudson, and Shaheen Jafargholi
First 'Three Tenors' concert featuringPlácido Domingo,José CarrerasandLuciano Pavarottiat Baths of Caracalla in Rome; recording becomes world's best-selling classical record
Otis Reddingrecords the song "Respect" at Stax Studios in Memphis, Tennessee; it later becomes a signature piece forAretha Franklin
Wimbledon Women's Tennis:Martina Navratilovawins her record ninth Wimbledon singles title, defeating AmericanZina Garrison6-4, 6-1
Baltimore Orioles owner Jack Dunn offers future baseball legendBabe Ruth, Ernie Shore & Ben Egan for $10k toConnie Mack(Philadelphia A's); refuses pleading lack of finance
Lisa Leslie, American WNBA center (LA Sparks)/forward (Olympic gold 1996), born in Gardena, California
Jon Pertwee, English actor and entertainer (Third Doctor in "Doctor Who"; 128 episodes, Worzel Gummidge), born in London (d. 1996)
Robert A. Heinlein, American sci-fi author (4 Hugos, Red Planet), born in Butler, Missouri (d. 1988)

Marc Chagall [Moise Shagal], Jewish Belarusan-French modernist painter and stained glass artist (La Mariée; I and the Village), born in Liozna, Russian Empire (now Belarus) (d. 1985) [some sources cite date as  July 6]
Toussaint Louverturedeclares Haitian independence
Nelson Mandelasteps down as President of South Africa
PresidentEisenhowersigned a bill approving Alaskan statehood
Hernán Cortésand the Tlaxcalans defeat a numerically superior Aztec force in the Battle of Otumba in Mexico
French Emperor Napoléon Bonaparte and Russian Tsar Alexander I meet on a raft in the middle of the Neman River and sign the first Treaty of Tilsit
Emperor Maximilian Iorders appointment of a conductor, two bass singers and six choir boys, establishing the Vienna Court Music Ensemble; later includes Vienna Boys Choir
All women board of directors takes control of ABA's Kentucky Colonels
Katipunan: the Revolutionary Philippine Brotherhood is established leading to the fall of the Spanish Empire in Asia
127°F (53°C), Parker, Arizona (state record until 1994)
South Africa beats Cameroon 1-0 in Durban in first FIFA sanctioned match after nearly 20 years international isolation, apartheid
Space shuttle STS-71 (Atlantis 14), lands
Space Shuttle STS 78 (Columbia 20), lands

NBA approves franchise swap; Buffalo Braves owner John Y. Brown and Harry Mangurian acquire Boston Celtics, while the Celtics owner Irv Levin gets Braves, later moved to San Diego to become the Clippers
Surrey wicket-keeper Ted Pooley completes a then-1st class cricket record 12 dismissals (8 caught, 4 stumped) in a County match against Sussex at The Oval
